summary: Retest of iteration 1 fixes for AUKTAVE 2K26. Both critical fixes VERIFIED. FIX 1 (banner/navbar layering): at viewport 375x812 on /about and /, after localStorage.clear()+reload, mobile-menu-toggle is now clickable without pointer-event interception from announcement-banner; data-testid=mobile-menu becomes visible after click. Banner now lives inside Navbar header in same fixed z-50 container (AnnouncementBanner uses z-10 inside header, no longer overlaps). Banner-dismiss works and persists across reload (localStorage auktave-banner-dismissed=1). FIX 2 (audio fallback): /audio/upside-down-fallback.mp3 HEAD returns 200 (~10MB self-hosted); audio element loads with readyState=4, error=null; no 4xx/pixabay audio requests; no console 403 audio errors. Smoke tests: / /about /events/hackathon /register /help /404 all load 200 or render 404 copy correctly; hero-register-cta navigates to /register; 404 page contains 'Hawkins'. One non-blocking minor: on homepage the audio-play-toggle button click is intercepted by the .vines-bg decorative div (absolute inset-0 inside main) because the click coordinates happen to be covered by that overlay despite AudioController being z-50 fixed - this does not affect audio loading (no 403), but prevents play via user click on the homepage.

ui_bugs.issue: Click on [data-testid='audio-play-toggle'] on / is intercepted by '<div class="absolute inset-0 vines-bg">' inside <main class="relative">. AudioController is fixed z-50 so it renders above visually, but the vines-bg overlay appears to catch pointer events at the bottom-right coordinates of the button. Audio itself loads fine (readyState=4, no errors), just can't be started by clicking. Suggested fix: add 'pointer-events-none' to the .vines-bg class (it is decorative only).

action_items: OPTIONAL (MEDIUM): Add pointer-events-none to .vines-bg (or any absolute inset-0 decorative overlays inside <main>) so that fixed-position controls like audio-play-toggle are reliably clickable on the homepage. Audio still works functionally (loads, no 403) - this only affects user interaction with play button on /.
critical_code_review_comments: Banner + navbar layering fix is clean - single fixed z-50 container in Navbar with AnnouncementBanner as first child at z-10 in document flow correctly pushes the nav row below the banner without overlap.

Audio fallback now self-hosted at /public/audio/upside-down-fallback.mp3 (10MB) - acceptable for a landing site but a 10MB fetch on every first visit is significant bandwidth. Consider hosting a smaller looped segment (~1-2MB) or lazy-loading after user gesture.
Decorative overlays inside <main> (.vines-bg, and possibly others) should always have pointer-events-none to avoid interaction bugs like the audio-play-toggle interception.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 1 CRITICAL bugs are both fixed. Only remaining minor issue is .vines-bg overlay intercepting the audio-play-toggle click on homepage. Web3Forms placeholder key still in place (intentional, surfaces register-error as expected). All 7 route smoke tests pass, 11 routes returning correct status codes. Banner dismiss persistence via localStorage 'auktave-banner-dismissed' verified. Audio fallback file exists at /app/frontend/public/audio/upside-down-fallback.mp3 (10MB, served 200).
rca of the issue: FIX 1 RCA confirmed: moving AnnouncementBanner INSIDE the Navbar <header class='fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-50'> as the first child (at z-10) means the banner and navbar row share one stacking context at z-50, and the banner is part of document flow (not absolute/fixed over the nav row). This causes the navbar row to naturally sit below the banner's 36px height, so the hamburger button coordinates are no longer under the banner. FIX 2 RCA confirmed: FALLBACK_AUDIO updated from Pixabay CDN (403) to local /audio/upside-down-fallback.mp3 (200, 10MB). Minor unresolved: .vines-bg absolute overlay intercepts clicks on homepage at audio-play-toggle coordinates - add pointer-events-none CSS.
